req.title: Hr-Admin deletes performance-review
req.statement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to delete a performance-review through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers: (1) happy-path deletion by an authenticated hr-admin (EP – valid partition), (2) deletion attempt by an unauthenticated user (NEG – 401 path), (3) deletion attempt by a role that exists but lacks permission (NEG/DT – 403 path), (4) boundary check where the performance-review ID is the minimum valid value / edge identifier (BVA), and (5) state-transition check verifying the record moves from 'active' to 'deleted' and an audit entry is created (STC). Five tests satisfy the ≥3 minimum while giving broad coverage of the stated error pathways.

tests.name: test_hr_admin_deletes_performance_review_success
preconditions: A performance-review with known id exists in state 'active'
actions: Click the Delete button for that review
Confirm the deletion in the confirmation dialog
Assert HTTP 200/201 response with entity id
Assert audit record created for the deletion
expected: Performance-review is removed from the list
Response status is 200 or 201 containing the entity id
Audit record exists for the deleted review
trace: R-WEB-023: positive outcome – review deleted, audit written, 200/201 returned
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_hr_admin_deletes_performance_review_success(
    hr_admin_page,        # <fixture>: authenticated Playwright page for hr-admin
    create_review,        # <fixture>: creates a review, yields its id, cleans up if not deleted
    audit_log_api,        # <fixture>: REST client scoped to audit service
):
    review_id = create_review(state="active")
    page = hr_admin_page

    page.goto("/performance-reviews")
    row = page.locator(f"[data-review-id='{review_id}']")
    row.locator("[data-action='delete']").click()
    page.locator("[data-testid='confirm-delete']").click()

    expect(page.locator(f"[data-review-id='{review_id}']")).not_to_be_visible()

    response = <helper_fn>.last_api_response(page)
    assert response["status"] in (200, 201)
    assert str(review_id) in str(response["body"])

    audit = audit_log_api.get_latest(entity_id=review_id, action="DELETE")
    assert audit is not None, "Audit record must be written on successful delete"

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_delete_performance_review
preconditions: No active session / user is not authenticated
A performance-review with known id exists
actions: Send DELETE request to /performance-reviews/{id} without auth token
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_delete_performance_review(
    api_client,           # <fixture>: plain requests Session, no auth
    create_review,        # <fixture>
    audit_log_api,        # <fixture>
):
    review_id = create_review(state="active")

    response = api_client.delete(f"/performance-reviews/{review_id}")
    assert response.status_code == 401

    audit = audit_log_api.get_latest(entity_id=review_id, action="DELETE")
    assert audit is None, "No audit record should be written for rejected request"

    # Confirm review still exists via privileged read
    still_exists = <helper_fn>.review_exists(review_id)
    assert still_exists, "Review must not be deleted after 401 rejection"

tests.name: test_forbidden_role_cannot_delete_performance_review
preconditions: User is authenticated with role 'employee' (not hr-admin)
A performance-review with known id exists
trace: R-WEB-023: error pathway – 403 if role forbidden
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_forbidden_role_cannot_delete_performance_review(
    employee_api_client,  # <fixture>: requests Session authenticated as role 'employee'
    create_review,        # <fixture>
    audit_log_api,        # <fixture>
):
    review_id = create_review(state="active")

    response = employee_api_client.delete(f"/performance-reviews/{review_id}")
    assert response.status_code == 403

    audit = audit_log_api.get_latest(entity_id=review_id, action="DELETE")
    assert audit is None, "No audit record should be written after 403"

    still_exists = <helper_fn>.review_exists(review_id)
    assert still_exists, "Review must not be deleted after 403 rejection"

tests.name: test_delete_performance_review_boundary_minimum_id
preconditions: A performance-review exists whose id equals the system minimum valid id (e.g. 1)
actions: Locate the review with minimum id
expected: Review with minimum id is successfully deleted
Response 200/201 contains the minimum entity id
Audit record written for minimum id
trace: R-WEB-023: boundary – minimum valid entity id must be handled correctly
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

MIN_VALID_ID = 1

def test_delete_performance_review_boundary_minimum_id(
    hr_admin_page,        # <fixture>
    ensure_review_exists, # <fixture>: idempotently ensures review with given id exists
    audit_log_api,        # <fixture>
):
    ensure_review_exists(review_id=MIN_VALID_ID, state="active")
    page = hr_admin_page

    page.goto("/performance-reviews")
    row = page.locator(f"[data-review-id='{MIN_VALID_ID}']")
    row.locator("[data-action='delete']").click()
    page.locator("[data-testid='confirm-delete']").click()

    expect(page.locator(f"[data-review-id='{MIN_VALID_ID}']")).not_to_be_visible()

    response = <helper_fn>.last_api_response(page)
    assert response["status"] in (200, 201)
    assert str(MIN_VALID_ID) in str(response["body"])

    audit = audit_log_api.get_latest(entity_id=MIN_VALID_ID, action="DELETE")
    assert audit is not None

tests.name: test_delete_transitions_review_state_from_active_to_deleted
actions: Verify initial state of review is 'active'
Verify review state transitions to 'deleted' (soft-delete) or record is absent
Verify audit record reflects state transition
expected: Review no longer appears in active list
Audit record captures before-state 'active' and after-state 'deleted'
trace: R-WEB-023: state transition – active -> deleted on successful hr-admin delete
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_delete_transitions_review_state_from_active_to_deleted(
    hr_admin_page,        # <fixture>
    create_review,        # <fixture>
    audit_log_api,        # <fixture>
    review_state_api,     # <fixture>: REST client to read review state
):
    review_id = create_review(state="active")

    initial = review_state_api.get_state(review_id)
    assert initial == "active", f"Pre-condition failed: expected 'active', got {initial!r}"

    page = hr_admin_page
    page.goto("/performance-reviews")
    page.locator(f"[data-review-id='{review_id}']").locator("[data-action='delete']").click()
    page.locator("[data-testid='confirm-delete']").click()

    expect(page.locator(f"[data-review-id='{review_id}']")).not_to_be_visible()

    final = review_state_api.get_state(review_id)
    assert final in ("deleted", None), f"Expected deleted state, got {final!r}"

    audit = audit_log_api.get_latest(entity_id=review_id, action="DELETE")
    assert audit is not None
    assert audit.get("before_state") == "active"
